title: crambe laevigata
intro: Crambe laevigata, also known as sea kale or horse kale, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Crambe laevigata is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, lowering blood pressure, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of crambe laevigata can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to aid in detoxification and boost the immune system.
